groups.title: The Elmsden Way
groups.desc:
Questions 11-14
Label the map below.
Write the correct letter, A-I, next to Questions 11-14.

Questions 15-20
Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.title: What warning does the speaker give about cycling on the Elmsden Way?

options.title: A
options.content: The roads usually have heavy traffic.

options.title: C
options.content: There may be animals on the cycle path.

questions.title: What does the speaker say about Elmsden Station?

options.title: A
options.content: It is a very busy station.

questions.title: In the Visitors' Centre, people can
If cyclists want to travel between Elmsden and Langton by train,

options.title: A
options.content: they need to book in advance.

options.title: B
options.content: they have to travel at the weekend.

options.title: C
options.content: they must pay an additional charge for their bicycle.

questions.title: What warning does the speaker give about walking to the River Elm?

options.title: A
options.content: It can take a long time to get back.

options.title: B
options.content: There is nowhere to leave bicycles.

options.title: C
options.content: The waterfall is inaccessible on foot.

questions.title: To find out about other cycle paths in the region, you can

options.title: C
options.content: ring up the national cycle network.

transcript.en: Welcome to our holiday special.
Today, I'll be talking about a new cycle route in this area called the Elmsden Way.
It's well sign-posted, so you can see exactly where to go, and it's an easy ride with no steep hills.
It's 35 kilometers long from Elmsden to Langton and consists partly of cycle paths and partly of roads.
Let me outline the route briefly, starting at Elmsden railway station.
The first part is a cycle path running eastwards roughly parallel to the rail track.
Almost immediately, the railway curves away and goes around the southern side of a little lake, but the cycle path takes the northern route around the lake.
Keep your eyes open for the swans that nest there.
The path then joins the road and the next landmark is a group of massive rocks that tower over the countryside.
You'll see them to the east of the road just before the road goes under the railway.
They're imposing.
Soon you'll come to a fork in the road where you'll leave the road and head off towards the river.
But if you want to make a detour to Corley nature reserve, go straight on instead of leaving the road here.
Continuing along the cycle path, you'll reach the river Cleve.
The cycle path follows the river southwards.
After a bit, you'll see the Ashington china factory, which closed in 1962.
It now offers guided tours to the public which is worth a visit.
Right after the factory, there's a bridge over the river and you carry on with farmland to the north right after the bridge and the Langton forest to the south.
Somewhere between the cycle route and the railway line sited a statue of Captain Cook, which dates back to the 18th century.
The route ends at Langton village, where you'll find a railway station.
So, you can either catch the train here or cycle back to Elmsden.
One point to remember when cycling on the Elmsden Way is to keep your eyes open for sheep, which occasionally stray onto the cycle path from the adjoining fields.
So be careful to avoid accidents.
The park is well-constructed, so wet or muddy conditions do not affect it.
There'll be old cars or tractors when you're cycling on roads, but most traffic has been diverted to the main road that bypasses the whole area.
Now a bit more about some of the places you'll see.
The starting point, Elmsden Station, was quite busy in the early 20th century with services to several major cities, but it was closed in the 1950s.

Eventually, a small company was set up to turn it into a sort of railway museum, maintaining it in the style of the 1950s, and the station was ready for trains to start using it again earlier this year.
It's only used by a few local trains, though.

There's a Visitors' Centre at the station, which houses a small exhibition about the history of railway transport all over the world, and a facility for hiring bikes if you don't have one of your own.
A small shop just across the road from the station also sells refreshments.
As I said, you can cycle to Langton and return to Elmsden by train or the other way because you can take your cycle on the train.
There's no need to make a reservation; there's plenty of room and no extra charge.
However, the service is limited, and the trains between these places don't run on weekdays.
If you want to see the River Elm, there's a footpath from Elmsden station.
And you can safely leave your bike in the car park there.
Leave yourself plenty of time, though.
Going down is easy, but climbing up is pretty steep, so walking back up to the top can take quite a while.
But it's worth the effort.
The scenery is lovely and the footpath runs right next to the river down to a waterfall.
Finally, there are many other cycle paths if you like to do more cycling in the area.
We'll be talking on the radio about them, and you'll be able to get fuller details in the Saturday edition of the local paper.
The national cycle network also has some details on its website, although I have to say they don't have any local ones yet.
But that may change, so get out your bicycle ...

11.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 rocks。
题目解析：在定位词 rocks 出现之前已经有一系列地标，考生需注意识别。首先听到 lake，天鹅在那里筑巢，因此可以排除 A。接着往下听，听到定位信息 rocks 出现，就在路的东面，穿过铁路之前的位置，因此答案为 B 而非 C。注意动态图文章中低频难点词 before、after 的用法。

12.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 Corley nature reserve。
题目解析：定位词 Corley nature reserve 出现后，听到“直走，不要离开公路”，而沿着公路直走只有一个位置被标注，那就是 E。

13.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 Ashington china factory。
题目解析：上一题结束后本题马上就开始了。先听到地标信息 river Cleve，自行车道沿河向南行进，随后听到本题定位信息 Ashington china factory，也就是沿河的下一个标注位置 F。注意动态路线不要跳跃式选择，根据地标，按照时间顺序以就近原则做出判断。

14.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 Langton forest。
题目解析：上一题通过描述性信息结束后，本题开始。先出现一座桥，继续骑行，过桥后，北面有一片农田，也就是 G，南面则是 Langton forest，而南面唯一标注的位置只有 I。

15.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 Elmsden Way，条件为 warning。
题目解析：先听到定位词 Elmsden Way，随后就听到条件信息 warning 被替换为 keep your eyes open，引出绵羊会走到自行车道上，对应 C 选项，因此 C 选项为正确答案。接着听到的避免事故，并不是 A 选项的 heavy traffic，而是避免撞到绵羊。之后听到 B 选项 Bad weather 的替换信息 wet or muddy conditions，但原文说的是道路建设得很好，不会受其影响，与 B 选项表达不符。最后又听到大部分车辆被分流至主干道，绕过了这个区域，可进一步确认 A 选项的 heavy traffic 不正确。

16.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 Elmsden Station。
题目解析：定位词 Elmsden Station 先出现，接着 A 选项的 busy 原词出现，但时间是在 20 世纪初期，与 A 选项的时态不符，因此可以排除 A 选项。之后听到该车站被改造成一个铁路博物馆，保持了 20 世纪 50 年代的风格，与 B 选项表达的“现代化”不符，因此可以排除 B 选项。最后听到 C 选项的替换信息 ready for trains to start using it again earlier this year，故 C 选项为正确答案。

17.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 Visitors' Centre。
题目解析：定位词 Visitors' Centre 先出现，接着先听到 C 选项中的 history 一词，但原文说的是铁路运输的历史，而非当地的历史，C 选项偷换概念，可以排除。继而听到和 A 选项几乎原词出现，信息完整，可以选择。最后听到 B 选项的 refreshments 原词出现，但其并非游客中心提供的，而是马路对面的商店售卖的，不符合题干要求，可以排除。注意，选择类题型中，替换只是现象，不要按照替换与否盲目判断对错，要把握考点。

18.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 Elmsden、Langton、train，条件为 cyclists。
题目解析：定位词先出现，接着听到 C 选项部分信息“可以带自行车上火车”，但未提及收费与否，需要继续往下听。先听到 no need to make a reservation，与 A 选项的表述相反，因此 A 选项可排除。随后听到 no extra charge，意味着 C 选项的“需要额外收费”信息为假，可以排除。最后听到 B 选项的替换信息“工作日不运行”，因此 B 选项为正确答案。

19.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 River Elm，条件为 warning、walking。
题目解析：先听到定位词 River Elm，随后听到可以把自行车安全停放在那儿的停车场，与 B 选项表述相反，可以排除。接着听到 A 选项的替换信息 take quite a while，因此 A 选项为正确答案。最后听到人行道可以通向瀑布，与 C 选项表述相反，可以排除。

20.analysis:
听前预测：定位词为 other cycle paths，条件为 in the region。
题目解析：定位词原词重现，接着听到 B 选项的 radio，但未提及选项中的 website。继续往下听，听到也可以从当地报纸的周六版上获取更详尽的资讯，对应 A 选项，因此 A 选项为正确答案。之后听到 C 选项的 the national cycle network 和 website，但未听到 C 选项中的打电话，且该网站还没有任何当地的骑行路线，不符合题干要求，可以排除。
